ops.body: Lane D protocol (workspace /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer-wd, $0 LLM). Degeneracy definition pinned from E26 artifacts (out/per_pair.csv + ledger 310454b94): the 7 probe-flagged degenerate pairs = 5 exact-identical (chosen==rejected) + 2 whitespace-only train pairs (d_word=0.0, d_qwen 0.0011/0.0015). The corpus has a clean gap above them: next-nearest pair sits at d_word 0.0079 / d_qwen 0.0057 (the 7 near-identical `direction` blocks, d~0.006-0.011, which are DPOP-risk but NOT degenerate per the probe). Filter: in export.py dpo view assembly, exclude rows where chosen == rejected or normalized word-level Levenshtein (E26's exact d_word sensitivity metric: WORD_RE r"\w+|[^\w\s]", rapidfuzz Levenshtein, d = dist/max(len)) < DPO_DEGENERACY_FLOOR = 0.005 — mid-gap, catches the degenerate cluster only. The Qwen primary tokenizer is not export-runtime-viable (transformers + HF cache); d_word flags the identical set of train degenerates. Note: end-to-end on the snapshot this drops 8 pairs, not 7 — the extra is test pair 3723c61f6343 (d_word=0.0, 1-char whitespace diff in 338 chars), the same degeneracy shape the probe's train-focused characterization did not enumerate. sft/kto untouched. Tests: seeded degenerate + healthy pair; dpo excludes exactly the degenerate one, sft/kto keep both; read-only snapshot recount as verification.
